dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - May 08, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**ASTROS DOMINATE REDS IN 10-0 SHUTOUT**

The Houston Astros rolled to a 10-0 victory over the Cincinnati Reds, led by Mike Burrows' dominant pitching and a trio of Astros hitters who each recorded multi-hit games. Burrows tossed 7 shutout innings, striking out 6 and walking just 1, while Zach Cole hit a home run and drove in 3 runs to pace the Astros' offense. The Reds were unable to get anything going, with Nick Lodolo taking the loss after allowing 3 runs in 5 innings.

**ROCKIES EDGE PHILLIES IN THRILLING 9-7 WIN**

The Colorado Rockies outlasted the Philadelphia Phillies 9-7, thanks in part to a big day from Hunter Goodman, who hit a home run and drove in 2 runs. Goodman finished with 4 hits, the most on the team. Tyler Freeman also had a strong game, driving in 2 runs. The Phillies' Kyle Schwarber hit a home run, but it wasn't enough to lift his team to victory. Jesús Luzardo pitched well for the Rockies, but Chase Dollander took the loss after allowing 2 earned runs in 5.2 innings.

**ATHLETICS EDGE ORIOLES IN 4-3 WIN**

The Athletics narrowly defeated the Baltimore Orioles 4-3, thanks in part to a strong outing from Kyle Bradish. Bradish tossed 7 innings, striking out 10 and allowing just 3 earned runs. Adley Rutschman hit a home run for the Orioles, but it wasn't enough to lift his team to victory. Jacob Lopez earned the win in relief, pitching 1.2 scoreless innings to secure the victory.

**BLUE JAYS SHUT DOWN ANGELS IN 2-0 WIN**

The Toronto Blue Jays blanked the Los Angeles Angels 2-0, behind a strong outing from Dylan Cease. Cease tossed 7 scoreless innings, striking out 10 and allowing just 5 hits. Kazuma Okamoto drove in the Blue Jays' only run with an RBI single.
